conclusion: Underwriter is SiriusPoint Specialty Insurance Corporation. Plan Administrator is International Medical Group (IMG). Master Policy effective October 1, 2025.
exact_language: SiriusPoint Specialty Insurance Corporation (the Company) promises and agrees to provide the Insured Person with the benefits described in the Master Policy... The Master Policy is effective as of October 1, 2025

conclusion: Period of Coverage limit: $50K/$100K/$250K/$500K/$1M (age ≤69); $50K/$100K (age 70–79); $10K (age 80+). Coverage period 5 days to 12 months.
section: BENEFIT SUMMARY — Coverage Limit / Maximum Amount

conclusion: Deductible options: $0, $100, $250, $500, $1,000, or $2,500 per insured person per Period of Coverage.
section: BENEFIT SUMMARY — Deductible for Eligible Medical Expenses
exact_language: Deductible $0, $100, $250, $500, $1,000 or $2,500 per Insured Person, as indicated on the Declaration

conclusion: In-network US: plan pays 100%, insured pays 0%, OOP max $0. Out-of-network US: plan pays 90%, insured pays 10%, OOP max $500. International: plan pays 100%, OOP max $0.
section: BENEFIT SUMMARY — Coinsurance for Eligible Medical Expenses
exact_language: Coinsurance Plan pays 100% [in-network] / Plan pays 90% [out-of-network] / Plan pays 100% [international] ... Out of Pocket Maximum $0 / $500 / $0

conclusion: Pre-existing conditions are EXCLUDED. Charges directly or indirectly from any Pre-existing Condition are not covered.
exact_language: Charges resulting directly or indirectly from or relating to any Pre-existing Condition are excluded from coverage under this insurance.

conclusion: Pre-existing condition = any injury/illness that existed at time of application OR any time during the 3 years prior to Effective Date. Lookback period: 3 years. Applies whether or not previously manifested, symptomatic, known, diagnosed, treated, or disclosed.
exact_language: Pre-existing Condition: Any Injury, Illness, sickness, disease, or other physical, medical, Mental or Nervous Disorder, condition or ailment that, with reasonable medical certainty, existed at the time of Application or at any time during the three (3) years prior to the Effective Date of this insurance, whether or not previously manifested, symptomatic or known, diagnosed, Treated, or disclosed to the Company prior to the Effective Date, and including any and all subsequent, chronic or recurring complications or consequences related thereto or resulting or arising therefrom.

benefit: Acute Onset of Pre-existing Conditions — Summary Coverage
conclusion: Acute Onset of Pre-existing Conditions covered up to the full Period of Coverage limit. Insured Person must be under 70 years of age. Emergency Medical Evacuation from Acute Onset limited to $25,000.
section: BENEFIT SUMMARY — Acute Onset of Pre-existing Conditions
exact_language: Acute Onset of Pre-existing Conditions • Up to the Period of Coverage limit • Insured Person must be under 70 years of age

conclusion: Acute Onset = sudden and Unexpected outbreak or reoccurrence that is of short duration, rapidly progressive, requires urgent medical care. Chronic/congenital conditions or gradually worsening conditions are NOT Acute Onset. Conditions for which the insured person knew treatment was forthcoming are excluded.
section: EE. DEFINITIONS — Acute Onset of Pre-existing Condition
exact_language: Acute Onset of Pre-existing Condition: A sudden and Unexpected outbreak or reoccurrence that is of short duration, is rapidly progressive, and requires urgent medical care. A Pre-existing Condition that is chronic or congenital, or that gradually becomes worse over time is not an Acute Onset of Pre-existing Condition. An Acute Onset of Pre-existing Condition does not include any condition for which, as of the Effective date, the Insured Person (i) knew or reasonably foresaw he/she would receive, (ii) knew he/she should receive, (iii) had scheduled, or (iv) were told that he/she must or should receive, any medical care, drugs or Treatment.

conclusion: Acute Onset coverage requires: (a) Treatment within 24 hours of outbreak; (b) insured under age 70; (c) not traveling against medical advice; (d) not traveling to seek treatment for pre-existing condition; (e) not in modified treatment program; (f) pre-existing condition must have been stabilized ≥30 days prior to Effective Date; (g) must be traveling outside Country of Residence.
section: I. ACUTE ONSET OF PRE-EXISTING CONDITIONS — CONDITIONS AND RESTRICTIONS
exact_language: (a) Treatment must be obtained within twenty-four (24) hours of the sudden and Unexpected outbreak or reoccurrence (b) the Insured Person must be under seventy (70) years of age ... (f) the Pre-existing Condition must have been stabilized for at least thirty (30) days prior to the Effective Date without change in Treatment (g) the Insured Person must be traveling outside their Country of Residence.

conclusion: ER for Injury: NOT subject to ER Deductible. In-network: 100%. Out-of-network: 90%.
section: BENEFIT SUMMARY — Hospital Emergency Room: United States
exact_language: Hospital Emergency Room: United States • Injury: Not subject to Emergency Room Deductible [Plan pays] 100% [in-network] 90% [out-of-network]

conclusion: ER for Illness: Subject to a $250 additional Deductible per ER visit if Treatment does NOT result in direct Hospital admission. If admitted, $250 ER deductible does not apply. In-network: 100%. Out-of-network: 90%.
section: BENEFIT SUMMARY — Hospital Emergency Room: United States
exact_language: • Illness: Subject to a $250 Deductible for each Emergency Room visit for Treatment that does not result in a direct Hospital admission [Plan pays] 100% [in-network] 90% [out-of-network]

conclusion: Inpatient hospitalization covered at average semi-private room rate including nursing services and ancillary services. In-network: 100%. Out-of-network: 90%. International: 100%.
exact_language: Hospitalization / Room & Board • Average semi-private room rate • Includes nursing services, miscellaneous and Ancillary services [Plan pays] 100% [in-network] 90% [out-of-network] 100% [international]

conclusion: ICU covered. In-network: 100%. Out-of-network: 90%. International: 100%.
exact_language: Intensive Care 100% [in-network] 90% [out-of-network] 100% [international]

conclusion: Surgery covered. In-network: 100%. Out-of-network: 90%. International: 100%. Reconstructive surgery covered when incidental to and following covered surgery. Assistant surgeon covered at 20% of primary surgeon's eligible fee.
section: BENEFIT SUMMARY — Surgery / Reconstructive Surgery / Assistant Surgeon
exact_language: Surgery 100% [in-network] 90% [out-of-network] 100% [international] / Reconstructive Surgery • Surgery is incidental to and follows Surgery that was covered under the plan 100% 90% 100% / Assistant Surgeon • 20% of the primary surgeon's eligible fee 100% 90% 100%

conclusion: Laboratory, Radiology/X-ray, Pre-admission Testing all covered at 100% in-network, 90% out-of-network, 100% international.
section: BENEFIT SUMMARY — Laboratory / Radiology / X-ray / Pre-admission Testing
exact_language: Laboratory 100% 90% 100% / Radiology / X-ray 100% 90% 100% / Pre-admission Testing 100% 90% 100%

conclusion: Prescriptions: NOT APPLICABLE in-network US (use in-network pharmacy = no separate Rx benefit, covered under eligible medical expenses). Out-of-network US: 90%. International: 100%. Dispensing max: 90-day supply per prescription. $250K sub-limit applies if plan max is $500K or $1M.
section: BENEFIT SUMMARY — Prescription Drugs and Medication
exact_language: Prescription Drugs and Medication Not Applicable 90% 100% • Obtained through Retail Pharmacy, Inpatient and Outpatient Surgery, Emergency Room and Outpatient Office Visits • Dispensing maximum for Retail Pharmacy: 90 days per prescription / If the Certificate of Insurance Maximum Limit is $500,000 or $1,000,000, the Prescription Drugs and Medications Maximum Limit is up to $250,000 per Period of Coverage

conclusion: Physical Therapy covered. In-network: 100%. Out-of-network: 90%. International: 100%. Requires medical order or treatment plan.
exact_language: Physical Therapy • Medical order or Treatment plan required 100% 90% 100%

conclusion: Home Nursing Care covered. In-network: 100%. Out-of-network: 90%. International: 100%. Must be provided by a Home Health Care Agency. Must follow direct transfer from acute care facility.
exact_language: Home Nursing Care • Provided by a Home Health Care Agency 100% 90% 100% • Upon direct transfer from an acute care Facility

conclusion: Durable Medical Equipment covered. In-network: 100%. Out-of-network: 90%. International: 100%.
exact_language: Durable Medical Equipment 100% 90% 100%

conclusion: Emergency Medical Evacuation: Maximum Limit $1,000,000. Must be approved in advance and coordinated by the Company. In-network, out-of-network, and international: 100%.
exact_language: Emergency Medical Evacuation • Maximum Limit: $1,000,000 • Approved in advance and coordinated by the Company 100% 100% 100%

conclusion: On Effective Date and renewal dates, insured must have legally departed Country of Residence (India for Indian nationals) and legally entered the Destination Country (USA). Cannot have established Habitual Residency in Destination Country.
exact_language: (5) on the Effective Date and on subsequent renewal dates, must have legally departed the Country of Residence and legally entered the Destination Country (6) not have established a Habitual Residency in the Destination Country

conclusion: Coverage can be extended minimum 5 days, up to maximum 24 continuous months (365-day increments). Extension Premium must be paid. Insured must continue meeting eligibility requirements.
section: CONDITIONS AND GENERAL PROVISIONS — RENEWAL; AMENDMENTS
exact_language: an Insured Person can request coverage under this insurance plan to be extended a minimum of five (5) days for up to a three hundred sixty-five (365) day period until reaching a maximum of twenty-four (24) continuous months in accordance with and subject to the Terms of the plan then in effect

conclusion: Plan uses a US PPO network. Treatment from in-network PPO provider = 100% (after deductible). Non-PPO = 90% with $500 OOP max. PPO directory available at www.imglobal.com/member. Specific PPO network name NOT stated in Certificate — NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW.
section: F. UNITED STATES PREFERRED PROVIDER ORGANIZATION (PPO)
exact_language: If Treatment or supplies eligible for coverage under this insurance are received directly from the Company's approved list of independent Preferred Provider Organization (PPO) providers while the Insured Person is in the United States, the Company will adjust the Deductible and/or Coinsurance applicable to such claims according to the amount shown in the BENEFIT SUMMARY... an Insured Person may visit the Plan Administrator's website at www.imglobal.com/member to obtain such information.
flag: NEEDS MANUAL REVIEW — specific PPO network name (UHC/First Health/MultiPlan) not stated in Certificate. Check IMG member portal.

conclusion: Pre-certification required for: Chemotherapy, Extended Care Facility, Home Nursing Care, Inpatient Hospitalization, Outpatient Surgical procedures, Interfacility Ambulance Transfer, Radiation Therapy, Surgery. Failure to pre-certify = 50% reduction of eligible medical expenses. Emergency admission: pre-certify within 48 hours.
exact_language: (a) Chemotherapy (b) Extended Care Facility (c) Home Nursing Care (d) Inpatient Hospitalization (e) Procedures rendered at an Outpatient Surgical Center or Outpatient Hospital (f) Interfacility Ambulance Transfer (g) Radiation Therapy (h) Surgery or Surgical procedure.

conclusion: Claims filed via MyIMG portal. 180-day filing deadline. Appeals within 90 days of denial. Inside US: +1.800.628.4664.
exact_language: Inside the United States: +1.800.628.4664 / Outside the United States: +1.317.655.4500

conclusion: COVID-19 / SARS-CoV-2 treatment is a covered Eligible Medical Expense.
exact_language: (11) Charges for Treatment resulting from COVID-19/SARS-CoV-2

conclusion: Urgent Care Clinic covered. NOT subject to Deductible. $25 copayment (waived if $0 Deductible selected). In-network: 100%. Out-of-network: 90%. International: 100%.
exact_language: Urgent Care Clinic • Not subject to Deductible • Copayment: $25 100% 90% 100% • Copayment is not applicable if the Declaration states a $0 Deductible
